name: walkie-mcp
description: 📻 walkie phase-3 IDLE-PUSH — an HTTP/SSE-transport MCP channels server (sidecar @D s15 1.8 allows HTTP/SSE MCP; STDIO MCP stays forbidden). walkie(0.3.0) + walkie-arm(0.1.1) capture inbound session-to-session lines into `~/.sidecar/walkie/<self>.inbox.log`, but an idle session only drains them on its NEXT turn (poll). This plugin closes that gap: a single local Node broker (Streamable HTTP on 127.0.0.1:7717) tails `<self>.inbox.log` from a dedicated `<self>.mcp.offset` (separate from walkie-arm's `.offset` so the two never contend) and, per new JSON line `{from,text,ts}`, emits an MCP channel notification `method:notifications/claude/channel` `params:{content:"📨 from <handle>: <text>"}` — which Claude Code PUSHES into the live-but-idle session (no turn needed). The server declares the `experimental["claude/channel"]` capability the host requires; plugin.json exposes it as a `channels` entry + an http url-based `mcpServers` entry (NOT stdio command-based → lint passes). receive-PUSH only — send stays the existing `/walkie call` socket path (no re-implementation). SessionStart fires a detached daemon-launcher (pidfile + pgrep guard, walkie-arm pattern; on first run `npm install --omit=dev @modelcontextprotocol/sdk` into the plugin dir if node_modules absent — node_modules never committed). sidecar's FIRST non-hexa (Node) executable. IDLE-PUSH requires Claude Code launched with `--dangerously-load-development-channels walkie-mcp` (or `--channels walkie-mcp` once channels GA) AND a first-party provider (Bedrock/Vertex skip) — experimental research-preview. NO opt-out switch (s11) — gates only on real preconditions (node present · handle set), else graceful exit 0 (never blocks session start). NO absolute paths ($HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only).
